enriched_points.point_title: Dispersion models vs. chemical transport models
enriched_points.expanded_content: Dispersion models primarily focus on how pollutants spread in the atmosphere, while chemical transport models (CTMs) also account for chemical transformations and interactions.
Statistical models utilize historical data and statistical techniques to predict air quality, offering insights where physical models may be complex or data-sparse.

enriched_points.point_title: Strengths and weaknesses of each approach
enriched_points.expanded_content: Dispersion models are simpler and faster but less accurate for chemical processes, whereas CTMs provide detailed simulations but require extensive data and computational resources.
Recent trends show a preference for hybrid approaches combining statistical and physical models, enhancing predictive capabilities while addressing data limitations and computational costs.
